payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active, current version of Sam's memory from Goldman; mem_old was filtered out as it is explicitly marked as superseded and therefore not valid for retrieval under the system's durability and consistency rules.
citations: memory_policy_v2.1: Section 4.3 - Superseded memories are excluded from recall unless explicitly requested via historical query

payload.decision: recall returned mem_new because it is the current, non-superseded version of Sam's memory from Goldman, while mem_old was filtered out as it has been explicitly superseded by mem_new and is no longer valid for retrieval

entity_resolution.reason: The system resolved 'Sam from Goldman' to the canonical entity ID representing the most recent, non-superseded memory state, which is mem_new

memory_cards.statement: mem_new contains the current, updated information about Sam from Goldman, including role, tenure, and project involvement as of the latest known state
mem_old contains outdated information about Sam from Goldman that was valid at an earlier time but has been replaced by mem_new due to a role change, project update, or data correction

details: Successfully identified mem_new as current and mem_old as superseded
Applied recall filter per memory policy: exclude superseded memories
Returned only the non-superseded memory card (mem_new)
No ambiguity remained in entity resolution — Sam from Goldman maps unambiguously to mem_new

repair_options: If historical context is needed, use a time-bounded recall query with a timestamp prior to the supersession event
